userStories.title: Migrate runtime from Node + tsgo to Bun
userStories.description: Replace the current build+run pipeline (tsgo compile then node execute) with Bun, which compiles and runs TypeScript natively. This eliminates the separate build step, simplifies the dev workflow, and speeds up execution. Bun is a drop-in Node replacement that supports ESM, TypeScript, and all Node APIs used by agent-cli (child_process, fs, path, etc.).
acceptanceCriteria: Replace npm scripts: dev/start use 'bun run src/index.ts' instead of tsgo build + node dist/index.js
Remove or demote tsgo and tsc build scripts (keep as fallback with build:old or build:tsc)
Verify all Node built-in modules used (child_process, fs, path, readline, etc.) work under Bun
Verify chalk and commander work under Bun
Update CLAUDE.md Build & Run section to reflect Bun as the primary runtime
npm run dev still works as the main development command
Build passes and agent-cli runs correctly with Bun
userStories.notes: tsgo is only a compiler (TS -> JS), not a runtime. Bun handles both compilation and execution natively, removing the build step entirely.

userStories.title: Fix stale branch detection to auto-update branchName instead of stopping
userStories.description: When the stale branch detection (US-010) finds that the branch in prd.json no longer exists, it currently clears branchName to empty string and stops the loop with an error — forcing the user to manually edit prd.json. Instead, it should detect the current git branch (git branch --show-current) and automatically update prd.json with it. If the current branch exists, continue the loop normally. If not on a branch (detached HEAD), only then stop with an error.
acceptanceCriteria: When branchName in prd.json no longer exists, detect current git branch via git branch --show-current
Auto-update prd.json branchName with the current branch instead of clearing it to empty string
Log a warning that the old branch was stale and the new branch was set
Continue the loop normally after updating — do not stop
If in detached HEAD state (no current branch), stop with an error as before
Archive the previous run before switching to the new branch
Build passes with npm run build
userStories.notes: Current behavior clears branchName and crashes. Found when switching from feat/new_tools (deleted) to feat/bun — had to manually edit prd.json. Depends on US-010 (stale branch detection).

userStories.title: Remove dead .last-branch mechanism from archiver
userStories.description: The .last-branch file is intended to detect branch changes between runs and trigger archiving, but it's broken by design: initialize() writes the current branch to .last-branch BEFORE checkAndArchive() reads it, so they always match and archiving on branch change never fires. Since stale branch detection (US-020) already handles the real-world case of switching branches, .last-branch is dead code. Remove the .last-branch file, the readLastBranch/writeLastBranch methods, and simplify checkAndArchive accordingly.
acceptanceCriteria: Delete the .last-branch file from the repo
Remove readLastBranch and writeLastBranch methods from Archiver
Simplify checkAndArchive to remove the branch comparison logic
Remove the lastBranchPath property and LAST_BRANCH_FILE constant
Remove the .last-branch write from initialize()
iterator.ts no longer references .last-branch behavior
Add .last-branch to .gitignore
npm run build passes
userStories.notes: The archiver still archives via handleStaleBranch() in iterator.ts, so removing .last-branch doesn't break the archiving use case. The branch comparison in checkAndArchive was dead logic.
